# CURRICULUM REASONING
# Generated by: [Account name / session date]
# Course: [ACTIVE COURSE]
# Lab: [ACTIVE LAB]
#
# PURPOSE:
# This file explains WHY the curriculum is ordered this way
# for THIS specific student. It is not generic advice.
# Every entry references the student's actual weakness
# patterns, misconceptions, and learning history.
#
# READ THIS before designing or executing any project.
# It changes which project comes next and how to teach it.
#
# NEXT 5 PROJECTS — detailed reasoning:
# (older projects summarized at bottom)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Student weakness : [specific weakness this targets]
  Risk if skipped  : [what breaks later without this]
  Teaching angle   : [what approach works for this student]
  Analogy to use   : [specific analogy that fits this student]
  What to watch for: [misconception likely to appear]
  Success signal   : [how you know it clicked]
